title: woof EPIC.md front-matter
description: YAML front-matter of .woof/epics/E<N>/EPIC.md. Captures Definition-stage commitments: observable outcomes, contract decisions, acceptance criteria. Free-form prose may follow the front-matter; only the structured header is validated. After Definition closes, outcome IDs and contract-decision IDs are append-only (per E1 rule); ID removal requires gate-driven deprecation.

epic_id.description: GitHub issue number. Same value as plan.json.epic_id.

title.description: Short epic title; mirrors gh issue title.

acceptance_criteria.description: Prose statements that, taken together, define epic completion.

open_questions.description: Discovery-stage questions that survived into Definition unresolved. Should be empty when Definition closes; non-empty signals a Stage 2 incompletion.

evidence.description: Prose strings naming surfaces (route, CLI, DB row, UI element). Stage 5 Check 4 greps for surface patterns in the diff.

deprecated.description: True iff this outcome was deprecated post-Definition via gate revision (per E1). Deprecated IDs remain in the array for traceability; new work targets a replacement ID.

replaced_by.description: When deprecated=true, the outcome ID that replaces this one. Optional; absent means deprecated outright.

epic_contract.description: Canonical surface declared by the epic (route, schema, data shape, user-visible string).

repo_convention.description: Pre-existing repo pattern, if different from epic_contract. Optional.

resolution.description: epic = canonical surface wins, repo convention not preserved. bridge = both kept, adapter declared. The 'repo' resolution is intentionally absent (E146 invariant).
